description_long: This hotel is a multi-story wooden structure with a traditional East Asian architectural style, featuring intricately layered, upturned tiled roofs and exposed timber framing. The facade includes a central main building flanked by two lower wings, all topped with dark gray, weathered roof tiles. The exterior walls are constructed from wooden planks and beams, with some sections showing latticework and window panels. At the front, there is a small covered porch area, and various props such as barrels, crates, and a wooden cart are placed nearby, suggesting a bustling, functional environment. The building is elevated on short stilts at one end, and the overall design emphasizes symmetry and craftsmanship. The muted color palette and visible wear give the hotel an authentic, historic appearance.
description: A traditional East Asian-style wooden hotel with layered, upturned tiled roofs, timber framing, and a weathered, historic look. The facade features a central entrance, lattice windows, and props like barrels and a cart.
description_view_0: This view highlights the side and rear of the hotel, showing the layered roof structure, timber framing, and the stilted section on the right.
description_view_1: The front view displays the main entrance, covered porch, and various props such as barrels, crates, and a wooden cart, emphasizing the hotel's welcoming facade.
description_view_2: This angle reveals the side elevation, the stilted support structure, and the continuation of the tiled roof and wooden paneling.
description_view_3: The rear view focuses on the symmetry of the rooflines and the back entrance, with less visible detail compared to the front.
attribute_entrance_canopy: wooden overhang integrated with the roof structure
